all.title: Power agency that destroyed Pacific Northwest wildfire evidence ‘completely rewrites’ mitigation plan for 2026
all.description: The Pacific Northwest’s federal power agency, recently found guilty of destroying evidence that may have tied it to a disastrous 2020 wildfire, has released its latest fire mitigation plan. The Bonneville Power Administration released this year’s updated plan on Wednesday, with officials saying the plan had been “completely rewritten” to elevate the agency’s risk mode

all.title: Water pollution lasts for years after a wildfire burns, new research shows
all.description: Contaminants from wildfires can create long term water quality concerns in areas far from the fire-impacted zones as drifting smoke plumes and rainfall runoff challenge standard water filtration techniques. A recently published analysis of 23 studies across 28 watersheds determined that the concentrations of numerous pollutants in waterways experienced significant increases for five years after [&

all.title: Wildfire science editing award
all.description: Congratulations to Dr Xinyan Huang, recipient of the International Journal of Wildland Fire Outstanding Associate Editor Award for 2025. This award honours Associate Editors who have demonstrated sustained excellence in their responsiveness, thoroughness, and timeliness. Dr Huang has served as an Associate Editor of the International Journal of Wildland Fire (IJWF) since 2021. He is […]

all.title: Canada begins its aerial firefighting build
all.description: Canada’s long-term pledge to build up its aerial firefighting resources has begun with the lease of 12 aircraft that will be shared nationally for the 2026 wildfire season. As reported in March, Canada has put aside $316.7 million over the next five years for the Canadian Interagency Forest Fire Centre (CIFFC) to manage a national […]

all.title: Extinction of unique US animals and plants at risk in wildfire on California’s ‘Galapagos of North America’
all.description: Extinction has always been a risk on Santa Rosa Island. Researchers have hailed the California island as having the most prolific aleontological record of extinct species such as pygmy mammoth, flightless sea duck, vampire bat and giant deer mouse. The environment is now home to animals and plants found nowhere else on Earth, earning its […]

all.title: Volunteer Handline Crews Model Gains Traction as Wildfires Increase in Germany
all.description: By Jan Sudmersen and Detlef Maushake Germany is a little smaller than California but has almost twice as many people, about 84 million. Though the country is densely populated and industrialized in large areas, about 30 per cent is defined as forest land – half the trees are conifers – and 50 per cent of […]

all.title: Wildfire’s history and future in the Great Plains on display in new website
all.description: Nebraska is still reeling from recent destructive wildfires and heading into what’s expected to be an active fire summer. A new website launched by the University of Nebraska–Lincoln seeks to put the state’s long history of fire on full display, and in turn, help farmers, ranchers and land managers restore the state’s native grasslands. The historic […]

all.title: Australia’s aerial firefighting operators don’t feel safe. Fire agencies disagree
all.description: Pilots are being pressured to fly into potentially dangerous fire scenarios or where aircraft would provide little benefit, despite worsening aerial firefighting safety risks, according to a newly released survey conducted by the Australian Transport Safety Bureau. The federal agency conducted interviews with nine firefighting aircraft operators and 11 fire agency representatives from around Austr

all.title: Record-low water shortages expected ahead of very active US wildfire season
all.description: The United States’ Natural Resources Conservation Service on Thursday issued a dire forecast for Western states, ahead of what’s expected to be a very active wildfire season. The Service, overseen by the Department of Agriculture, said many of the states’ rivers are expected to have extremely low streamflows for the spring and summer seasons. “Many […]

all.title: Wildfire camp sets up in Germany
all.description: For the first time in its 70-year history the large Interschutz fire and rescue services trade fair in Germany will feature a zone dedicated to wildfire.   Wildfire Camp will display around 20 companies and other organizations on early detection, prevention and suppression of wildfires. While small in relation to the rest of the trade fair, […]

all.title: Smoke, heat, rain and flooding; what to expect from Western weather
all.description: From B.C. to Manitoba, weather warnings are in effect through Sunday. From heavy rain and flooding, to wildfire smoke and high heat, residents are urged to plan for conditions.
